---
title: "CB3 Equity Report — Housing & Affordability Maps"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: cb3
---

## Purpose

This notebook reads the clean Housing & Affordability tract table and maps five
representative dimensions:

1. Severe rent burden: `severe_rent_burden_pct`
2. Crowded housing: `crowded_households_pct`
3. Subsidized properties at risk: `subsidized_units_expiring_2025_2030`
4. New affordable housing: `new_affordable_units_since_2018`
5. Housing quality: `hpd_open_class_bc`

Each metric is presented on its own map, as a bubble layer sized by magnitude
over selectable demographic choropleth base layers. Map HTML files are written
to `maps/housing_and_affordability/`.

The notebook also reads the clean CB3 tract baseline profile and creates one
selectable-layer demographics map for median household income, age, race and
ethnicity, poverty, and limited-English-proficient households.

```{python}
# Import packages for tract data, geometry, classification, and interactive maps.
from pathlib import Path
import sys
import yaml

import folium
import geopandas as gpd
import pandas as pd

# Ensure the reusable scripts package imports whether this QMD is rendered from
# the project root or from scripts/.
for candidate in [Path.cwd(), *Path.cwd().parents]:
    if (candidate / "src" / "map_utils.py").exists():
        sys.path.insert(0, str(candidate))
        break

from src.map_utils import (
    add_bubble_layer,
    add_map_title,
    add_metric_layer,
    add_zero_value_legend,
    find_project_dir,
    make_base_map,
)
```

```{python}
# Define project inputs and output locations. Resolve the project root so the
# notebook works whether it is rendered from the root folder or from scripts/.
PROJECT_DIR = find_project_dir()
CLEAN_DATA_PATH = (
    PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "clean" / "housing_and_affordability_tract.csv"
)
BASELINE_PROFILE_PATH = (
    PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "clean" / "cb3_tract_baseline_profile_clean.csv"
)
SUBSIDIZED_POINTS_PATH = (
    PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "clean" / "housing_and_affordability_subsidized_points.csv"
)
NEW_CONSTRUCTION_POINTS_PATH = (
    PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "clean" / "housing_and_affordability_new_construction_points.csv"
)
TRACT_GEOMETRY_PATH = (
    PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "raw" / "Geography" / "2020_Census_Tracts_20260505.geojson"
)
MAP_OUTPUT_DIR = PROJECT_DIR / "maps" / "housing_and_affordability"
MAP_OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

# metadata schema for the colors and units for each metric
with open(PROJECT_DIR / "docs" / "metric_metadata.yml") as f:
    ALL_METRICS = yaml.safe_load(f)
```

## Prepare tract geometry and metrics

```{python}
# Read the clean metrics with GEOID preserved as an 11-character string.
metrics = pd.read_csv(CLEAN_DATA_PATH, dtype={"GEOID": "string"})

# Read the 2020 tract polygons, normalize GEOID, and retain only the 31 clean rows.
tract_geometry = gpd.read_file(TRACT_GEOMETRY_PATH)
tract_geometry["GEOID"] = tract_geometry["geoid"].astype("string").str.zfill(11)
tract_geometry = tract_geometry[["GEOID", "geometry"]]

# Join the metrics to polygons and verify a complete one-to-one match.
tracts = tract_geometry.merge(
    metrics,
    on="GEOID",
    how="inner",
    validate="one_to_one",
)
tracts = gpd.GeoDataFrame(tracts, geometry="geometry", crs=tract_geometry.crs)

assert len(tracts) == 31
assert tracts["GEOID"].is_unique
assert set(tracts["GEOID"]) == set(metrics["GEOID"])

# Read tract-level demographics and join them to the same 31 tract polygons.
# The demographics file currently has 30 tracts, so one tract remains mapped
# as "Not available" in demographic layers.
baseline_profile = pd.read_csv(BASELINE_PROFILE_PATH, dtype={"geoid": "string"})
baseline_profile["GEOID"] = baseline_profile["geoid"].str.zfill(11)

DEMOGRAPHIC_COLUMNS = [
    "GEOID",
    "median_household_income",
    "age_0_to_19_share",
    "age_20_to_64_share",
    "age_65_plus_share",
    "white_non_hispanic_share",
    "black_non_hispanic_share",
    "asian_non_hispanic_share",
    "hispanic_share",
    "poverty_rate",
    "senior_poverty_rate",
    "lep_household_share",
]

tracts = tracts.merge(
    baseline_profile[DEMOGRAPHIC_COLUMNS],
    on="GEOID",
    how="left",
    validate="one_to_one",
)

# Read building-level subsidized-housing-at-risk points (native lat/lon, not
# collapsed to a tract centroid) and attach tract/NTA labels for tooltips.
subsidized_points = pd.read_csv(SUBSIDIZED_POINTS_PATH, dtype={"GEOID": "string"})
subsidized_points = subsidized_points.merge(
    tracts[["GEOID", "tract_label", "nta_name"]],
    on="GEOID",
    how="left",
    validate="many_to_one",
)

# Read building-level new-construction-since-2018 points (native lat/lon, not
# collapsed to a tract centroid) and attach tract/NTA labels for tooltips.
new_construction_points = pd.read_csv(NEW_CONSTRUCTION_POINTS_PATH, dtype={"GEOID": "string"})
new_construction_points = new_construction_points.merge(
    tracts[["GEOID", "tract_label", "nta_name"]],
    on="GEOID",
    how="left",
    validate="many_to_one",
)
```

```{python}
# Build METRIC_SPECS and DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS from metric_metadata.yml.
# To change a palette, label, or unit, edit the YAML — not this notebook.
def _to_map_spec(meta, include_filename=False):
    spec = {
        "dimension": meta["short_label"],
        "label":     meta["label"],
        "unit":      meta["unit"],
        "palette":   meta["palette"],
    }
    if include_filename:
        spec["filename"] = meta["filename"]
    for key in ("positive_values_only", "zero_color", "zero_label"):
        if key in meta:
            spec[key] = meta[key]
    return spec

METRIC_SPECS = {
    var: _to_map_spec(meta, include_filename=True)
    for var, meta in ALL_METRICS.items()
    if meta["domain"] == "housing_and_affordability"
}

DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS = {
    var: _to_map_spec(meta)
    for var, meta in ALL_METRICS.items()
    if meta["domain"] == "demographics"
}

for metric in METRIC_SPECS:
    assert metric in tracts.columns, f"{metric} missing from tracts"
for metric in DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS:
    assert metric in tracts.columns, f"{metric} missing from tracts"
```

```{python}
# Reuse shared mapping helpers from scripts/map_utils.py. Keep this small wrapper
# so the rest of the notebook can call base_map() without passing tracts each time.
def base_map():
    return make_base_map(tracts)

```

```{python}
def create_single_metric_map(
    metric,
    points=None,
    lat_col="tract_centroid_latitude",
    lon_col="tract_centroid_longitude",
    tooltip_fields=["tract_label", "nta_name"],
    tooltip_aliases=["Census tract", "NTA"],
):
    # Choropleth layer: demographic fills, selectable one at a time (radio
    # buttons), for geographic/demographic context. Point layer on top: one
    # bubble sized by magnitude. By default the bubble sits at the tract
    # centroid, since most metrics here are only reported at the tract level.
    # Metrics with native, finer-grained coordinates (e.g. individual building
    # addresses) should pass their own ``points``/``lat_col``/``lon_col``
    # instead, since collapsing them to a tract centroid would discard real
    # location detail.
    spec = METRIC_SPECS[metric]
    map_object = base_map()

    for index, demographic in enumerate(DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS):
        add_metric_layer(
            map_object, tracts, demographic, DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS[demographic],
            show=index == 0, add_legend=False, overlay=False,
        )

    add_bubble_layer(
        map_object,
        tracts if points is None else points,
        metric,
        spec["label"],
        spec["unit"],
        name=spec["dimension"],
        lat_col=lat_col,
        lon_col=lon_col,
        tooltip_fields=tooltip_fields,
        tooltip_aliases=tooltip_aliases,
        show=True,
        overlay=True,
    )
    add_map_title(
        map_object,
        f"CB3 Housing & Affordability: {spec['dimension']}",
        "Choropleth: use the layer control to pick a demographic fill. "
        f"Bubbles: {spec['label']}.",
    )
    add_zero_value_legend(
        map_object,
        "#d9d9d9",
        "Demographic data not available",
    )
    folium.LayerControl(collapsed=False, position="topright").add_to(map_object)
    output_path = MAP_OUTPUT_DIR / spec["filename"]
    map_object.save(output_path)
    return map_object
```

## Separate maps

### Severe rent burden

```{python}
severe_rent_burden_map = create_single_metric_map(
    "severe_rent_burden_pct"
)
severe_rent_burden_map
```

### Crowded housing

```{python}
crowded_housing_map = create_single_metric_map(
    "crowded_households_pct"
)
crowded_housing_map
```

### Subsidized properties at risk

```{python}
# Subsidized housing is reported at the building level, not the tract level,
# so bubbles use each building's own coordinates rather than a tract centroid.
subsidized_housing_at_risk_map = create_single_metric_map(
    "subsidized_units_expiring_2025_2030",
    points=subsidized_points,
    lat_col="latitude",
    lon_col="longitude",
    tooltip_fields=["standard_address", "tract_label"],
    tooltip_aliases=["Address", "Census tract"],
)
subsidized_housing_at_risk_map
```

### Subsidized properties at risk (2031–2040)

```{python}
subsidized_housing_at_risk_2031_2040_map = create_single_metric_map(
    "subsidized_units_expiring_2031_2040",
    points=subsidized_points,
    lat_col="latitude",
    lon_col="longitude",
    tooltip_fields=["standard_address", "tract_label"],
    tooltip_aliases=["Address", "Census tract"],
)
subsidized_housing_at_risk_2031_2040_map
```

### New affordable housing

```{python}
# New construction is reported at the building level, not the tract level, so
# bubbles use each building's own coordinates rather than a tract centroid.
new_affordable_housing_map = create_single_metric_map(
    "new_affordable_units_since_2018",
    points=new_construction_points,
    lat_col="latitude",
    lon_col="longitude",
    tooltip_fields=["standard_address", "tract_label"],
    tooltip_aliases=["Address", "Census tract"],
)
new_affordable_housing_map
```

### Housing quality

```{python}
housing_quality_map = create_single_metric_map(
    "hpd_open_class_bc"
)
housing_quality_map
```

## Demographic context map

```{python}
# Add demographic context as selectable layers. The missing tract in the
# baseline profile is retained and shown in gray as "Not available".
demographics_map = base_map()
for index, metric in enumerate(DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS):
    add_metric_layer(
        demographics_map, tracts, metric, DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS[metric],
        show=index == 0, add_legend=False, overlay=False,
    )

add_map_title(
    demographics_map,
    "CB3 tract baseline demographics",
    "Use the layer control in the upper-right to select income, age, race/ethnicity, poverty, or limited-English-proficient household layers.",
)
add_zero_value_legend(
    demographics_map,
    "#d9d9d9",
    "Demographic data not available",
)
folium.LayerControl(collapsed=False, position="topright").add_to(demographics_map)

demographics_output_path = MAP_OUTPUT_DIR / "tract_baseline_demographics_layers.html"
demographics_map.save(demographics_output_path)
demographics_map
```

## Demographics with subsidized housing at risk (point layer)

```{python}
# Choropleth layer: demographic fills, selectable one at a time (radio buttons).
# Point layer on top: one circle per at-risk building, sized by the square root
# of subsidized_units_expiring_2025_2030 so bubble *area* tracks unit count.
# Buildings use their own address coordinates rather than a tract centroid.
demographic_bubble_map = base_map()

for index, metric in enumerate(DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS):
    add_metric_layer(
        demographic_bubble_map, tracts, metric, DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS[metric],
        show=index == 0, add_legend=False, overlay=False,
    )

subsidized_spec = METRIC_SPECS["subsidized_units_expiring_2025_2030"]
add_bubble_layer(
    demographic_bubble_map,
    subsidized_points,
    "subsidized_units_expiring_2025_2030",
    subsidized_spec["label"],
    subsidized_spec["unit"],
    name="Subsidized units expiring 2025-2030",
    tooltip_fields=["standard_address", "tract_label"],
    tooltip_aliases=["Address", "Census tract"],
    color="#252525",
    show=True,
    overlay=True,
)
subsidized_2031_2040_spec = METRIC_SPECS["subsidized_units_expiring_2031_2040"]
add_bubble_layer(
    demographic_bubble_map,
    subsidized_points,
    "subsidized_units_expiring_2031_2040",
    subsidized_2031_2040_spec["label"],
    subsidized_2031_2040_spec["unit"],
    name="Subsidized units expiring 2031-2040",
    tooltip_fields=["standard_address", "tract_label"],
    tooltip_aliases=["Address", "Census tract"],
    color="#a63603",
    show=True,
    overlay=True,
    legend_bottom_offset=145,
)

add_map_title(
    demographic_bubble_map,
    "CB3 demographics with subsidized housing at risk",
    "Choropleth: use the layer control to pick a demographic fill. Bubbles: "
    "size shows subsidized units at risk at each building, split into two "
    "selectable layers by expiration window (2025-2030, 2031-2040).",
)
add_zero_value_legend(
    demographic_bubble_map,
    "#d9d9d9",
    "Demographic data not available",
)
folium.LayerControl(collapsed=False, position="topright").add_to(demographic_bubble_map)

demographic_bubble_output_path = (
    MAP_OUTPUT_DIR / "demographics_with_subsidized_housing_at_risk.html"
)
demographic_bubble_map.save(demographic_bubble_output_path)
demographic_bubble_map
```

## Output validation

```{python}
# Verify that all HTML deliverables were written.
expected_map_files = [
    MAP_OUTPUT_DIR / spec["filename"] for spec in METRIC_SPECS.values()
] + [
    demographics_output_path,
    demographic_bubble_output_path,
]

for map_file in expected_map_files:
    assert map_file.exists()
    assert map_file.stat().st_size > 10_000

print(f"Wrote {len(expected_map_files)} map files to {MAP_OUTPUT_DIR}")
```
